full_text_rationale: Full text describes Traversal-as-Policy distilling OpenHands execution logs into a persistent executable Gated Behavior Tree with experience-grounded gate updates, spine memory, and SWE-bench/OpenHands evaluation.
extractor_notes: Exact-title prior extraction `li2026traversalaspolicy` reused after search text/title/path check; architecture_status=arch_skill_rule_library. Controlled-comparison status remains prior-prefilled pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Peiran Li, Jiashuo Sun, Fangzhou Lin, Shuo Xing, Tianfu Fu, Suofei Feng, Chaoqun Ni, Zhengzhong Tu
titulo: Traversal-as-Policy: Log-Distilled Gated Behavior Trees as Externalized, Verifiable Policies for Safe, Robust, and Efficient Agents
formato_armazenamento: Gated Behavior Tree (GBT): árvore executável single-rooted com 21 task-family roots. Cada nó macro = (description NL, coarse tags, risk_level, behavior_signature σ = (σ_disc, σ_cont), env_context pointer). Gates determinísticos: RuleGates (code predicates sobre ctx estruturado) + ContentGates (frozen Llama-Guard-3-8B classifier, temp=0). Spine memory = sequência compacta de macros visitados como estado de longo prazo. Self-evolution (GBT-SE): repair local via analogical successes com regression testing preservando safety invariants.
llm_utilizado: GPT-4o (primary base model), Llama-3-8B, Qwen3-VL-8B-Thinking (executor decoupling); Llama-Guard-3-8B (ContentGate classifier)
benchmarks_utilizados: SWE-bench Verified (500), WebArena (812), GPQA (448), AgentSafetyBench, AgentHarm (public), Agent Security Bench (ASB)
metricas_desempenho: Success Rate (SR%), Coverage (Cov%), Violation Rate (Viol%), Unsafe Success (USucc%), Tok/Chars (thousands), AgentSafetyBench score, HarmScore, ASB metrics
baseline_comparado: OpenHands CodeActAgent (gpt-4o native), +Global guardrail only, +GBT-Basic, +GBT-SE (ablation ladder progressiva)
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: GBT cresce via self-evolution (novos children, gates); spine memory é compacta per-episode (visitados macros apenas); tree é artefato persistente cross-benchmark
reducao_tokens: -39% Tok (208k→126k) e -40% Chars (820k→490k) no SWE-bench Verified; -45% Tok (94k→52k) e -43% Chars (360k→205k) no WebArena
resultado_principal: GBT-SE eleva success rate de 34.6% para 73.6% no SWE-bench Verified e de 19.7% para 66.9% no WebArena, enquanto reduz violations para 0.2% e unsafe success para 0.0%. Tokens reduzidos ~40%. Com 8B executors (Qwen3-VL-8B), GBT mais que triplica success no SWE-bench (14.0%→58.8%), demonstrando decoupling entre reasoning capacity offline e execução online.
desempenho_vs_baseline: +39.0pp SWE-bench (73.6% vs 34.6%); +47.2pp WebArena (66.9% vs 19.7%); +28.5pp GPQA (87.3% vs 58.7%); violations 2.8%→0.2% SWE-bench; unsafe success 1.2%→0.0%
custo_vs_baseline: Tok/Chars 208k/820k→126k/490k SWE-bench (-39%/-40%); 94k/360k→52k/205k WebArena (-45%/-43%); 8B executors viáveis com same GBT artifact
limitacoes: Coverage boundary explícito: fora de coverage (covered=0), GBT só garante primitive-level safety; distillation de logs pode herdar vieses dos trajectórios OpenHands; 21 task families fixas podem não cobrir todos domínios; gates dependem de structured ctx que pode omitir estado relevante
ameacas_validade: GPT-4o como base model primário; GBT distilado de logs OpenHands pode não transferir para outros frameworks; safety gates determinísticos sobre ctx podem falhar se ctx omitir campos relevantes; avaliação Protocol A (cross-benchmark distillation) pode ter overlap semântico residual; macro abstraction stability testada mas não garantida para todos cenários
